facts: **Panama** are the only team out of all 48 sides at this tournament to finish without scoring a single goal. — *BBC*
Harry Kane's goal made him **England**'s all-time tournament top scorer with 11 goals, passing Gary Lineker's record of 10. — *BBC*
Kane and David Beckham are the only two Englishmen to score at three different editions of the tournament. — *The Athletic*
**Croatia** described as "growing in confidence with each game, like a dad at a wedding." Captain Modrić (40) delivered an inch-perfect corner for Vlašić's 83rd-minute winner. *(Men in Blazers)*
Ghana reached the knockout stage for the first time since 2010, when they famously came within a Luis Suárez handball of a semi-final.
Austria reached the knockout stage for the first time since 1982 — a 44-year wait.
Sasa Kalajdzic's 96th-minute equaliser was the latest goal to change a team's tournament fate in group-stage history, rescuing Austria from elimination with the last kick of the match.
Riyad Mahrez scored Algeria's go-ahead goal in the 93rd minute, only for Austria to respond two minutes later — both teams advanced and Iran were eliminated.
Lionel Messi became the first player in history to score in seven consecutive tournament matches, a streak spanning from Australia in the 2022 round of 16 through all three 2026 group games.
Messi extended his own all-time scoring record at the tournament to 19 goals, the most by any men's player in its history.
The previous record of six consecutive tournament games with a goal was shared by Messi, Just Fontaine (1958), and Jairzinho (1970).
Davinson Sánchez had a 89th-minute header ruled out by VAR for offside by the narrowest of margins — described as 'the length of a toe' — denying Colombia a dramatic winner.
Colombia dominated with 24 shots to Portugal's 13 and an expected goals margin of 1.63 to 0.69, yet the match ended 0-0.
Prior to this tournament, **Canada** had lost every single match in their tournament history. Jesse Marsch: "Canadian heroes for the future children of this country." *(Men in Blazers)*

Stephen Eustáquio scored the stoppage-time winner. He lost his mother to brain cancer and his father to a heart attack one year later. Marsch: "I think from somewhere his parents are looking down, and they saw that." *(Men in Blazers)*
This is the first-ever Round of 32 match at this tournament — the opening game of the expanded knockout format.
Both nations are playing in a knockout match for the first time in their history.

**Paraguay** coach Gustavo Alfaro after the upset: "We dance to the music we're given. Sometimes it's cumbia, sometimes tango, sometimes rumba… today, polka." The Paraguayan government declared a spontaneous national holiday. *(Men in Blazers)*
Described as "like seeing Stone Cold Steve Austin use the Rock Bottom to beat The Rock" — **Germany**'s first penalty shootout loss in 44 years; they'd previously won their last 6. *(Men in Blazers)*
This was **Germany**'s first knockout match in 12 years — eliminated in the group stage at both 2018 and 2022. The last time they played a knockout game, they won the 2014 final.
**Germany** scored 10 goals in the group stage from seven different players, the most balanced attack at this tournament.
Deniz Undav scored three goals without starting a single group match — the tournament's most prolific substitute.
**Paraguay**'s Miguel Almirón was suspended after receiving the first-ever red card for covering his mouth — a new rule at this tournament.
**Paraguay**'s last knockout appearance was 2010, when they beat **Japan** on penalties in the R16 before losing to **Spain** in the quarterfinals.
This match falls exactly 32 years to the day since **Netherlands** beat **Morocco** 2-1 at USA '94 — also in the group stage of a tournament held in the United States. — *BBC*
Current **Netherlands** coach Ronald Koeman started that 1994 match against **Morocco**. — *BBC*
The **Netherlands** are three-time finalists (1974, 1978, 2010) but have never won the tournament. — *ESPN*
**Morocco** reached the semi-finals in 2022, the best finish by an African nation in tournament history. — *BBC*
**Morocco**'s Ismael Saibari has scored in every group match — one goal in each of their three games. — *ESPN*

Three **Morocco** players — Sofyan Amrabat, Noussair Mazraoui, and Anass Salah-Eddine — were born in the **Netherlands**. Both Amrabat and Salah-Eddine represented Dutch youth sides before switching allegiance. — *The Athletic*

**Japan** have never won a knockout match in tournament history, losing all four attempts: Turkey in 2002 (0-1), Paraguay in 2010 (penalties), Belgium in 2018 (2-3), and Croatia in 2022 (penalties). — *ESPN*
**Japan** reached the knockouts without key players Wataru Endo and Kaoru Mitoma, who both missed the entire tournament through injury. — *BBC*
Vinícius Jr scored in all three group matches — joining Jairzinho, Romário, Ronaldo, and Rivaldo as the only Brazilians to do so at a single tournament. — *ESPN*
**Japan** stunned **Brazil** 3-2 in a friendly in October 2025 in Tokyo, coming from 2-0 down — their first-ever win over the five-time champions. — *BBC*

Their only previous tournament meeting was the 2006 group stage in Dortmund, where **Brazil** won 4-1 with a Ronaldo double. That **Japan** squad had six players in European leagues; this one has just three domestic-based. — *The Athletic*
**France** playing "Champagne football, coq au vin football and ratatouille football" — their attack described as "more stacked than the 1992 Dream Team." *(Men in Blazers)*

Ousmane Dembélé scored the second-fastest hat-trick in tournament history against **Norway**, behind only Erich Probst in 1954. His four goals came from an xG of just 0.97 — the tournament's biggest overperformance. *(The Athletic)*
Michael Olise has the most assists (3) and through balls (7) of any player at the tournament so far. *(BBC Sport)*
Manager Didier Deschamps missed the **Norway** match following the death of his mother; he returned for the knockouts. Mbappé ran to embrace him after the match. *(The Athletic)*
**Sweden**'s best tournament finish was runners-up in 1958 as hosts, but they have not won a knockout match since beating **Switzerland** in the 2018 round of 16. *(BBC Sport)*

Antonio Nusa became **Norway**'s youngest-ever tournament goalscorer — nicknamed the "Norwegian Neymar." **Ivory Coast**'s Amad Diallo scored "one of the goals of the tournament" as a substitute. *(Men in Blazers)*

**Côte d'Ivoire** are playing in a knockout match for the first time in their history, having been eliminated in the group stage in all three previous appearances (2006, 2010, 2014). The golden generation of Drogba, Yaya Touré, and Kolo Touré never managed it. *(BBC Sport)*
Haaland has 60 international goals in 53 caps, the best goals-per-game ratio of any player with 50+ international goals in the last century. *(The Athletic)*
All five of Haaland's tournament goals have been one-touch finishes; all 14 shots across three games were first-time efforts. *(BBC Sport)*

**Mexico** have been eliminated in the round of 16 in seven consecutive tournaments (1994-2022), a streak known as the 'fifth game curse.' This Round of 32 is a new format — but the pressure to break through remains. — *Fox Sports*
**Mexico** have not conceded a first-half goal in 15 consecutive tournament matches, stretching back to 2014. — *The Athletic*
**Ecuador** have never won a knockout match — their only appearance was a 1-0 loss to **England** in the 2006 round of 16. — *ESPN*

English pubs have served 5.5 million extra pints during the tournament. James Horncastle rated his fear level at 6/10 pre-match — "would actually be higher if we were not playing in an air-conditioned dome." *(Men in Blazers, Horncastle)*

**Congo DR**'s Yoan Wissa is nicknamed "Kovo" (the bold one) back home — he scored 3-4 of their tournament goals. Their super fan Luma the Jet, who stands perfectly still during games, couldn't get a visa. *(Men in Blazers, Rog)*

Kane's two goals (75', 86') made him the first **England** player to score twice in a tournament knockout match since Lineker vs Cameroon in 1990. He now has 13 tournament goals — joint 6th all-time, level with Pelé. *(BBC Sport, Phil McNulty)*
Kane's second goal was clocked at 94kph — he knocked the ball away from Mbemba, chased it, and blasted it into the near top corner. GK Mpasi didn't move a muscle. *(The Athletic, Jack Pitt-Brooke)*

Clint Dempsey compared **Bosnia** to the 1990s Charlotte Hornets — "not going to win the whole thing, but on their day they had players who could just go off and hurt you." *(Men in Blazers, Dempsey)*
Keegan-Michael Key pre-match: "There's a fluidity that I have never seen an American team have before." He credited Pochettino: "The discipline sets you free." *(Men in Blazers, Keegan-Michael Key)*

The **USA**'s win was only their second-ever knockout victory — first since 2002 — and the first ever in primetime on home soil. It was also the first time the US has won three games at a single tournament. *(The Athletic, Paul Tenorio)*
Balogun's three tournament goals tie him for 2nd on the all-time US list with Landon Donovan (2010). Only Bert Patenaude's four in 1930 are more. *(The Athletic, Henry Bushnell)*
Tillman waved off Pulisic — who wanted to take the 82nd-minute free kick — and curled it in himself from 25 yards to seal the win with 10 men. *(The Athletic, Tom Bogert)*

Keegan-Michael Key, a self-described "proper Belgian," called the current squad "a bunch of big hunks back to try and commit one last crime" — comparing it to "a Kevin James movie." *(Men in Blazers, Keegan-Michael Key)*
**Belgium** rallied from 2-0 down — the biggest comeback of the 2026 tournament. Lukaku and Tielemans scored within 159 seconds (87' and 89') to force extra time. *(The Athletic, Tim Spiers)*

Tielemans' 125th-minute penalty is the latest goal in tournament history. He calmly slotted it into the top corner after a 7-minute delay when Senegal's Pathe Ciss went down on the penalty spot. *(BBC Sport, Adwaidh Rajan)*
Sarr scored his 4th goal in 4 matches — two shy of Mbappé's tournament-leading 6. He scored 21 goals for Crystal Palace last season. *(The Athletic, Tim Spiers)*
Croatia have reached the final in two of the last three tournaments (2018, 2022), winning four knockout matches in that run.

Portugal reached the semi-finals in 2006 (beating Netherlands and England en route) and the quarterfinals in 2022 (beating Switzerland 6-1), but have also fallen in the round of 16 in 2010 (Spain) and 2018 (Uruguay).
Austria have not won a knockout match since the 1954 tournament, when they beat hosts Switzerland 7-5 in the quarterfinals — the highest-scoring match in tournament history.
Spain have won 5 of their last 6 knockout matches at major tournaments.
Algeria have never won a knockout match — their only appearance was a 2-1 extra-time loss to Germany in the 2014 round of 16.

Switzerland have reached the knockout stage in four of the last five tournaments (2006, 2014, 2018, 2022) but have never won a knockout match at this tournament — losing in the round of 16 each time (Ukraine on penalties in 2006, Argentina in 2014, Sweden in 2018, Portugal 1-6 in 2022).
Cabo Verde are making their tournament debut and will be playing in a knockout match for the first time — one of the smallest nations (population ~600,000) ever to reach this stage.
Argentina have won 9 of their last 11 knockout matches at this tournament, including the entire 2022 title run.

Ghana have not won a knockout match since 2010, when they beat the USA 2-1 in extra time to reach the quarterfinals — where Asamoah Gyan's missed penalty after Luis Suárez's deliberate handball denied them a historic semi-final.
Colombia reached the quarterfinals in 2014 with James Rodriguez winning the Golden Boot, but were eliminated in the round of 16 in 2018.

Egypt are playing in a knockout match for the first time in their tournament history — despite being the most successful African nation in continental competition (7 Africa Cup of Nations titles), they have never advanced past the group stage.
Australia have never won a knockout match — losing to Italy (2006) and Argentina (2022) in the round of 16.
**Canada**'s Stephen Eustaquio scored the winning goal against South Africa in the 92nd minute of the Round of 32 — the latest winner **Canada** have ever scored at a finals. — *Sports Mole*

**Morocco** completed 801 passes against the Netherlands in their Round of 32 win — only **Spain** have managed more than 800 passes in a single tournament match since data collection began. — *Sports Mole*

**Morocco** have beaten **Canada** in all three of their previous meetings, including a 2-1 group-stage win at the 2022 tournament; **Canada** have never scored more than one goal against the Atlas Lions. — *Al Jazeera*
**France** have scored 13 goals in four matches at this tournament; at Euro 2024 they scored just four across the entire competition — including two own goals and a penalty. — *Sports Mole*

Michael Olise's five assists at this tournament are the most by any player at a single finals since 1994, while Ousmane Dembele's four goals include a hat-trick against Norway in the group stage. — *FIFA.com*

**Paraguay** defeated **Germany** to reach this stage, overcoming a 31-place gap in the pre-tournament rankings — the fourth-largest ranking deficit ever overcome in a finals knockout match since 1994. — *Yahoo Sports*

**Brazil** have lost two confirmed starters ahead of the **Norway** match — Raphinha (thigh) and Lucas Paquetá (thigh/hamstring) — making this Ancelotti's most injury-disrupted selection of the tournament; Casemiro's fitness is also uncertain after he was withdrawn late in the 2-1 win over Japan. — *RotoWire*
Bruno Guimarães leads the entire 2026 tournament with four assists, a total only surpassed for **Brazil** at a single edition by Pelé, who set up six in 1970. — *Opta Analyst*

**Norway**'s 2-1 win over Ivory Coast was their first-ever victory in a tournament knockout match; prior to 2026, they had played just two knockout games at major tournaments, losing both to Italy — in 1938 and 1998. — *Opta Analyst*

**Mexico** are only the fourth team in tournament history to win their first four matches without conceding a goal, and their unbeaten run at the Estadio Azteca in tournament play now stands at 10 matches (W8, D2) dating back to 1970. — *Football Whispers*

Julián Quiñones became only the fifth Mexican player ever to score three goals at a single tournament with his strike against Ecuador, and the second after Luis Hernández (four in 1998) to reach that mark at one edition. — *Fox Sports*

Harry Kane's brace against DR Congo made him the first **England** player to score two goals in a single tournament knockout game since Gary Lineker in the 1990 quarter-final against Cameroon; his 13 career tournament goals have moved him past Pelé's career total of 12. — *ESPN*

**Portugal** defeated **Spain** on penalties after a 2-2 draw in last summer's Nations League final — the pair's most recent competitive meeting — but across their 40-plus all-time internationals, **Spain** lead 18 wins to **Portugal**'s 7. — *Racing Post*
Mikel Oyarzabal has been directly involved in 23 goals in his last 16 starts for **Spain** and scored in two different knockout matches at this edition, the first Spanish player to do so. — *ESPN*

Cristiano Ronaldo's penalty against Croatia took his official career total to 976 goals, leaving him 24 away from becoming the first player in history to reach 1,000 competitive goals; he is also the first player to score in six different tournament editions. — *Fox Sports*

Folarin Balogun is only the third player in tournament knockout history to score in a match and be sent off, following Ronaldinho against England in 2002 and Zinedine Zidane against Italy in the 2006 final; his suspension leaves **USA** without their three-goal leading scorer. — *Fox News*

**Belgium** trailed Senegal 2-0 as late as the 85th minute of their Round of 32 match — the latest into any tournament game in normal time a side has recovered from a two-goal deficit — before Lukaku, Tielemans and Tielemans again sealed a 3-2 extra-time win. — *Opta Analyst*

**USA**'s 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina was their first tournament knockout-stage victory since 2002, the last time they reached the quarter-finals; a win against **Belgium** would match that 2002 run. — *ESPN*
Lionel Messi became the first player in history to score seven goals in two separate tournaments when he found the net against Cape Verde in the Round of 32. — *Sports Mole*
**Argentina** and **Egypt** have met just once on record — a 2-0 friendly in 2008 that Messi missed through injury — making this only their second-ever encounter. — *Goal.com*
**Egypt** have now conceded in six consecutive matches after previously keeping three clean sheets in a row, a defensive vulnerability **Argentina** will look to exploit. — *Sports Mole*

**Switzerland** made history at this tournament by winning three consecutive knockout-round matches for the very first time in their history, with the trio of Manzambi, Embolo, and Vargas combining for nine goal contributions. — *Tips.GG*
**Colombia** striker Jhon Córdoba was ruled out for the rest of the tournament after suffering a hamstring injury in just the 8th minute of the Round of 32 win over Ghana. — *Sports Mole*

**Colombia** defeated **Switzerland** 2-0 in the group stage at the 1994 tournament — the only prior meeting between the sides at a major tournament — a result revisited heavily in build-up to this fixture. — *Juvefc.com*

The only previous competitive meeting between **France** and **Morocco** was the 2022 semi-final, which **France** won 2-0 with Théo Hernández scoring inside five minutes and Randal Kolo Muani sealing it late on. — *The Football Matrix*
Michael Olise has registered five assists in the tournament for **France**, leaving him just three short of the all-time single-edition assists record. — *Goal.com*

**Morocco** midfielder Azzedine Ounahi ended an 11-game run without a goal at the finals when he scored twice against Canada in the last 16; all eight of his international goals for the Atlas Lions have come in victories. — *Goal.com*

**Belgium** last reached the quarter-finals of the finals in 2018, when they finished third in Russia; this is only their third quarter-final appearance in tournament history, and Rudi Garcia's side can match the fabled 1986 squad that eliminated **Spain** at the same stage. — *beIN Sports*

Kevin De Bruyne had played the full 90 minutes in 13 consecutive tournament appearances before being substituted in each of his first four matches at this edition, then dropped entirely for the last-16 against the United States — he is expected to return to the starting XI at Los Angeles Stadium. — *Sports Mole*

**Spain** have progressed from all six major knockout matches under Luis de la Fuente and are on track to become both reigning European and world champions simultaneously for the second time in their history, having first achieved the feat by pairing Euro 2008 with the 2010 title. — *Squawka*

**Norway**'s five matches at this tournament have produced 21 goals in total — an average of 4.2 per game — making them the highest-scoring side per match among the remaining eight teams. — *Goal.com*

Jarell Quansah's two-match suspension, Jordan Henderson's wrist surgery, a doubtful Marc Guehi and Reece James's three-game hamstring absence mean **England** manager Thomas Tuchel has used at least five different right-back solutions across the tournament. — *ESPN*
**Norway** have never beaten a European opponent in a tournament match; **England** have lost five of their last six knockout matches against European sides at this stage. — *Sports Mole*

Lionel Messi missed two penalties in the same tournament edition against Austria and Egypt — the only player in history to do so — yet still became the first player to score in six consecutive knockout matches. — *Opta Analyst*

**Switzerland** reached the quarter-finals for the first time since 1954 — a wait of 72 years — after beating **Colombia** 4-3 on penalties following a goalless draw, with goalkeeper Gregor Kobel making the decisive stop. — *ESPN*

**Argentina** became the first team in tournament history to overturn a two-goal deficit from as late as the 78th minute and win in regulation time, completing the comeback against Egypt through Enzo Fernández's 93rd-minute winner. — *ESPN*

**Lamine Yamal**, aged 18, is the top scorer in the **France** vs **Spain** fixture across all competitions with 3 goals in 2 matches, and he and **Pau Cubarsí** made **Spain** only the second side to start two teenagers in a tournament quarter-final since Brazil's Pelé and Altafini in 1958. — *ESPN*

**France** recorded an xG of 3.69 against Morocco in the quarter-final while conceding just 0.14 xG — a margin of dominance that head coach Didier Deschamps has never previously produced in a knockout match at this stage. — *ESPN*

**Spain** manager Luis de la Fuente is unbeaten in all 13 of his matches at major tournaments, and a win tomorrow would make him only the third manager of a European side to win each of his first seven knockout matches at this stage after Italy's Vittorio Pozzo and fellow Spaniard Vicente del Bosque. — *Squawka*
